Subject: Health and Human Development
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - Concepts of health
ContentDescription: In this area of study, students take a broad, multidimensional approach to health and wellbeing.

Subject: Health and Human Development
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - Concepts of health
Elaboration: Such an approach acknowledges that defining and measuring concepts of health are complicated by a diversity of social and cultural contexts.

Subject: Health and Human Development
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - Concepts of health
Elaboration: Students consider the measurable indicators of population health and look at data reflecting the health status of young Australians.

Subject: Health and Human Development
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - Concepts of health
Elaboration: Focusing on youth, students inquire into the reasons for variations and inequalities in health status, including the sociocultural factors that contribute to variations in health outcomes.

Subject: Health and Human Development
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - Concepts of health
ContentDescription: On completion of this unit, the student should be able to explain multiple dimensions of health and wellbeing, explain indicators used to measure health status and analyse sociocultural factors that contribute to variations in the health status of youth.

Subject: Health and Human Development
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - Concepts of health
ContentDescription: To achieve this outcome, the student will draw on the key knowledge and key skills outlined in Area of Study 1.

Subject: Health and Human Development
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Youth health and wellbeing
ContentDescription: In this area of study, students apply the broad concepts of health and wellbeing from Area of Study 1 to their study of Australia’s youth.

Subject: Health and Human Development
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Youth health and wellbeing
Elaboration: They identify major health inequalities impacting Australia’s youth and reflect on the causes.

Subject: Health and Human Development
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Youth health and wellbeing
Elaboration: Students inquire into how governments and organisations develop and implement youth health programs and consider factors that influence the implementation of and access to these programs.

Subject: Health and Human Development
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Youth health and wellbeing
ContentDescription: Students conduct a research investigation and apply research skills to find out what young people are most focused on and concerned about regarding health outcomes.

Subject: Health and Human Development
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Youth health and wellbeing
Elaboration: The focus for this research could include key areas such as mental health and wellbeing, smoking and vaping, alcohol and other drugs, gambling, relationships and sexuality, and safety (for example, on the road, in the water and the sun, and online).

Subject: Health and Human Development
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Youth health and wellbeing
ContentDescription: Students select a particular focus area and conduct research, interpret data and draw conclusions on how the health of Australia’s youth can be promoted and improved.

Subject: Health and Human Development
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Youth health and wellbeing
ContentDescription: On completion of this unit, the student should be able to interpret data to identify key areas for improving youth health and wellbeing, and analyse one youth health area in detail.

Subject: Health and Human Development
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Youth health and wellbeing
ContentDescription: To achieve this outcome, the student will draw on the key knowledge and key skills outlined in Area of Study 2.

Subject: Health and Human Development
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 3 - Health and nutrition
ContentDescription: In this area of study, students explore food and nutrition as foundations for good health.

Subject: Health and Human Development
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 3 - Health and nutrition
Elaboration: They investigate the roles and sources of major nutrients and the use of food selection models and other initiatives to promote healthy eating.

Subject: Health and Human Development
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 3 - Health and nutrition
Elaboration: Students explore the health consequences of nutritional imbalance, especially for youth, and consider the sociocultural and commercial factors that influence the food practices of, and food choices made, by youth.

Subject: Health and Human Development
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 3 - Health and nutrition
Elaboration: They develop strategies for building health literacy and evaluating nutrition information from various sources, including advertisements and social media.

Subject: Health and Human Development
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 3 - Health and nutrition
ContentDescription: On completion of this unit, the student should be able to apply nutrition information, food selection models and initiatives to evaluate nutrition information.

Subject: Health and Human Development
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 3 - Health and nutrition
ContentDescription: To achieve this outcome, the student will draw on the key knowledge and key skills outlined in Area of Study 3.

Subject: Health and Human Development
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - Developmental transitions
ContentDescription: In this area of study, students examine the developmental transitions from youth to adulthood, with a focus on expected changes, significant decisions, and protective factors including behaviours.

Subject: Health and Human Development
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - Developmental transitions
Elaboration: They consider perceptions of what it means to be a youth and an adult and investigate the expected physical and social changes.

Subject: Health and Human Development
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - Developmental transitions
Elaboration: They inquire into factors that influence both the transition from youth to adulthood and later health status.

Subject: Health and Human Development
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - Developmental transitions
Elaboration: They consider the characteristics of respectful, healthy relationships.

Subject: Health and Human Development
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - Developmental transitions
Elaboration: Students examine parenthood as a transition in life.

Subject: Health and Human Development
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - Developmental transitions
Elaboration: With a focus on the influence of parents or carers, and families, they investigate factors that contribute to development, and health and wellbeing during the prenatal, infancy and early childhood stages of the human lifespan.

Subject: Health and Human Development
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - Developmental transitions
Elaboration: Health and wellbeing is considered as an intergenerational concept; that is, the health and wellbeing of one generation affects the next.

Subject: Health and Human Development
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - Developmental transitions
ContentDescription: On completion of this unit, the student should be able to explain developmental changes in the transition from youth to adulthood, analyse factors that contribute to healthy development during the prenatal and early childhood stages of the human lifespan and explain health and wellbeing as an intergenerational concept.

Subject: Health and Human Development
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - Developmental transitions
ContentDescription: To achieve this outcome, the student will draw on the key knowledge and key skills outlined in Area of Study 1.

Subject: Health and Human Development
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Youth health literacy
ContentDescription: In this area of study, students investigate the health system in Australia from the perspective of youth and their rights and responsibilities.

Subject: Health and Human Development
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Youth health literacy
Elaboration: They examine the functions of various entities that play a role in our health system.

Subject: Health and Human Development
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Youth health literacy
Elaboration: Students inquire into equity of access to health services, as well as the rights and responsibilities of youth receiving health care.

Subject: Health and Human Development
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Youth health literacy
Elaboration: They research the range of health services in their communities and suggest ways of improving the health literacy and health outcomes of youth.

Subject: Health and Human Development
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Youth health literacy
ContentDescription: On completion of this unit, the student should be able to explain factors affecting access to Australia’s health system that contribute to health literacy and promote the health and wellbeing of youth.

Subject: Health and Human Development
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Youth health literacy
ContentDescription: To achieve this outcome, the student will draw on the key knowledge and key skills outlined in Area of Study 2.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia’s health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - Understanding health and wellbeing
ContentDescription: In this area of study, students explore health and wellbeing, and illness as complex, dynamic and subjective concepts.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia’s health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - Understanding health and wellbeing
Elaboration: They reflect on both the universality of public health goals and the increasing influence of global conditions on Australians.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia’s health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - Understanding health and wellbeing
Elaboration: Students develop their understanding of the indicators used to measure and evaluate health status, and the factors that contribute to variations in health status between different groups.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia’s health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - Understanding health and wellbeing
ContentDescription: On completion of this unit, the student should be able to explain the complex, dynamic and global nature of health and wellbeing, interpret and apply Australia’s health status data, and analyse variations in health status.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia’s health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - Understanding health and wellbeing
ContentDescription: To achieve this outcome, the student will draw on the key knowledge and key skills outlined in Area of Study 1.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia’s health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Promoting health in Australia
ContentDescription: In this area of study, students look at different approaches to public health over time, with an emphasis on changes and strategies that have succeeded in improving health outcomes.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia’s health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Promoting health in Australia
Elaboration: They examine the progression of public health in Australia since 1900, noting global changes and influences such as the Ottawa Charter for Health Promotion, and the general transition of focus from the health and wellbeing of individuals to that of population groups including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Peoples.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia’s health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Promoting health in Australia
Elaboration: Students investigate the Australian health system and its role in promoting health and wellbeing.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia’s health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Promoting health in Australia
Elaboration: They apply their understanding of successful health promotion campaigns, programs and case studies to evaluate the ability of initiatives to identify priorities and improve health outcomes in Australia.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia’s health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Promoting health in Australia
ContentDescription: On completion of this unit, the student should be able to explain changes to public health approaches, analyse improvements in population health over time and evaluate health promotion strategies and initiatives.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia’s health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Promoting health in Australia
ContentDescription: To achieve this outcome, the student will draw on the key knowledge and key skills outlined in Area of Study 2.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Health and human development in a global context
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - Global health and human development
ContentDescription: In this area of study, students explore similarities and differences in health status and human development in low-, middle- and high-income countries, including Australia.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Health and human development in a global context
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - Global health and human development
Elaboration: They investigate a range of factors that contribute to health inequalities and study the concepts of sustainability and the Human Development Index to further their understanding of health and human development in a global context.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Health and human development in a global context
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - Global health and human development
Elaboration: Students inquire into the effects of global trends on health and human development.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Health and human development in a global context
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - Global health and human development
ContentDescription: On completion of this unit, the student should be able to analyse similarities and differences in health status and human development globally and analyse the factors that contribute to these differences.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Health and human development in a global context
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - Global health and human development
ContentDescription: To achieve this outcome, the student will draw on the key knowledge and key skills outlined in Area of Study 1.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Health and human development in a global context
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Health and the Sustainable Development Goals
ContentDescription: In this area of study, students look at action for promoting health globally.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Health and human development in a global context
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Health and the Sustainable Development Goals
Elaboration: They consider the importance of and relationships between the UN’s SDGs, focusing on their promotion of health and human development.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Health and human development in a global context
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Health and the Sustainable Development Goals
Elaboration: Students investigate the goal and objectives of the WHO and evaluate Australia’s aid program and the role of non-government organisations.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Health and human development in a global context
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Health and the Sustainable Development Goals
Elaboration: They reflect on meaningful and achievable individual and social actions that could contribute to the work of national and international organisations that promote health and wellbeing.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Health and human development in a global context
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Health and the Sustainable Development Goals
ContentDescription: On completion of this unit, the student should be able to analyse the relationships between the SDGs and their role in the promotion of health and human development and evaluate the effectiveness of global aid programs.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Health and human development in a global context
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Health and the Sustainable Development Goals
ContentDescription: To achieve this outcome, the student will draw on the key knowledge and key skills outlined in Area of Study 2.
